name: Secrets of Antares
short_description: Explore a breathtaking alien world in VR with just your jetpack and curiosity. No combat, no goals—just peaceful walking, flying, and soaking in the beauty of Antares. From tranquil beaches to sudden storms, it’s a serene escape shaped by stunning views and shifting weather.
detailed_description:
Secrets of Antares is a walking (and flying) simulator designed purely for peaceful exploration. No combat. No crafting. No objectives. Just you, a jetpack, and an alien planet full of breathtaking beauty — and raw, untamed nature. This isn't a game about action. It’s about presence.

Wander or fly freely through an alien world orbiting the red supergiant Antares — from paradise-like beaches and glowing caves to towering peaks and harsh weather-swept wilderness. There’s nothing to do but exist and explore. Most of the time, you don’t even need your hands — just drift, breathe, and let go.

Key Features:

  • Pure Freedom
    No missions, no UI, no distractions. Explore vast environments at your own pace. This is a retreat, not a challenge.

  • Jetpack-Driven Exploration
    Walk, hover, or soar across diverse biomes using a smooth, comfortable jetpack system built for VR. Unlock upgrades to reach floating islands, sky bridges, and remote corners of the world.

  • Stunning Visuals
    Powered by next-gen VR technology, every landscape is crafted for immersion — from lush forests and crystal-clear lagoons to bioluminescent caves and dynamic cloudscapes.

  • A Planet of Contrasts
    Experience moments of calm in paradise... or find yourself drifting through sudden alien storms, heavy rains, and other atmospheric events that bring the planet to life.

  • Effortless Relaxation
    No controls to master. No items to manage. Just move and exist. Ideal for winding down, meditating, or escaping into a peaceful alien dreamscape.

  • Always Something to See
    Enjoy a real-time day/night cycle, glowing wildlife, and weather that shapes your surroundings — whether you’re lying on a quiet beach or watching a storm roll in across the mountains.

If you’ve ever wished for a VR experience that simply lets you be — in a place that’s not Earth — Secrets of Antares was made for you.

Lukasz Jedrak 

Developer of Secrets of Antares
